I treated Turn 7‚Äôs relation as the only serious surviving candidate and found a new obstruction. The Turn 7 candidate is not correct.

The candidate was sigma,tau,x,y nx = (x*tau^85667662078)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1i.e.r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąí2)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,ŌÄ=ŌÄ2.This respects the prompt syntax: ŌÉ,ŌĄ are the distinguished tame generators, ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2 is implicit, and ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905(modM), ‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175(modM).The prior file‚Äôs Turn 7 reported many successful finite tame cup-rank tests for this relation, so I focused exactly there.

The new bad quotient isőĒ91,12=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČ91=1,ŌÉňČ12=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©.

This is a legal tame quotient because 91=7‚čÖ13‚ą£212‚ąí1=4095.The first-rank calculation still passes: the induced open subgroup hasdimF2H1=ef+2=91‚čÖ12+2=1094.So this is not a linear-rank failure. The failure is in the quadratic Magnus form, i.e. the DemuŇ°kin cup-product layer.For the Turn 7 relation, after eliminating the linear relation on a nontrivial ŌĄ-character block, the quadratic block attached to a nontrivial 91-st root őĪ has the following exact form. Put s=ŌÄmod12=9,u=(1+őĪ)‚ąí1.On the 12-dimensional ŌÉ-orbit block, indexed by c‚ąąZ/12Z, the matrix isBőĪ (c,c)=1+u2c+1+u2c+s,BőĪ (c,c+s)=BőĪ (c,c‚ąís)=1,with all other entries zero, where the off-diagonal terms are interpreted modulo 12. This formula is the useful new reduction: it compresses the full 1094-dimensional cup-product check to exact finite-field row reductions on 12√ó12 blocks.

Now take őĪ to be a root over F2of

P(T)=T12+T8+T6+T5+T4+T3+1.

This polynomial divides T91‚ąí1, and TmodP(T) has order 91. Exact row reduction overF2 [T]/(P(T))gives rankBőĪ=9,not 12. Thus the quadratic cup-product form has a nonzero radical on this tame-character block.

More globally, factoring T91‚ąí1 over F2, the nontrivial ŌĄ-part has dimension (91‚ąí1)‚čÖ12=1080,but the Turn 7 relation gives rank only936 on that part. Adding the trivial ŌĄ-part contributes the expected 12+2=14, so the total cup rank is at most 936+14=950,where the DemuŇ°kin group for the actual degree-1092 local field must have nondegenerate cup product of rank 1092+2=1094.So Turn 7‚Äôs candidate fails badly, although it passed all smaller tests reported there. The first missed obstruction occurs at mixed unramified period f=12, not at the earlier prime or 2-power periods.The source of the failure is now clearer. The ŌĄ‚ąí2 correction fixes the previously bad (5,4), (15,4), and related Frobenius-orbit blocks, but whenf=22‚čÖ3 the idempotent shift s=ŌÄmodf=9 splits the ŌÉ-cycle into three 4-cycles, and the diagonal terms1+u2c+1+u2c+9 do not prevent a radical on certain order-91 tame-character blocks. I also checked simple replacements x‚ąí1(xŌĄa)ŌÄ with a=¬Ī1,¬Ī2,¬Ī4,¬Ī8; ‚ąí2 is still the best of those, and the others fail earlier. So the next repair is probably not just changing ŌĄ‚ąí2 to another fixed ŌĄa.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 7‚Äôs strengthened candidate is refuted. In the legal tame quotient (e,f)=(91,12), its nontrivial ŌĄ-character quadratic block has rank 9 instead of 12 for roots of T12+T8+T6+T5+T4+T3+1. Hence the induced cup product is degenerate, impossible for the relevant local DemuŇ°kin group.

GAP: A replacement relation is still unknown. The missing term must fix mixed ŌÉ-periods such as f=12, not merely prime periods, 2-power periods, or the previously tested tame ramification cases.

NEXT: Use the block formulaBőĪ (c,c)=1+u2c+1+u2c+s,BőĪ (c,c¬Īs)=1 as a design constraint. Search for an additional (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-mixed correction whose character-block matrix kills the order-91 radical while preserving the already-correct (3,2),(5,4),(7,3),(15,4),(17,8),(31,5) tests.[BREAKTHROUGH]
